entries.title: What is Dependency Confusion
entries.body:
Dependency Confusion (a dependency substitution attack, also known as supply chain substitution) happens when an install script is tricked into downloading a malicious file from a public repository instead of the intended file with the same name from a company's internal (private) repository.

The gist: the package manager sees a package in the public registry with the same name as a private one but with a higher version, and pulls exactly that public (malicious) package. This is how the attacker's code ends up in the victim's build or environment.

entries.title: The attack idea and where to find private package names
entries.body:
Look for npm, pip, gem packages - the methodology is the same: you register a public package with the same name as a private one used by the company, and wait until it gets pulled in.

Where to look for private package names:
- Dependency manifests in source code and artifacts: package.json, composer.json, requirements.txt, pom.xml, Dockerfile.
- Leaked or public builds, frontend JS bundles, build errors in CI, caches.

Algorithm: list all dependencies from the manifests -> find the ones that are not in the public registry -> register a public package with the same name.

entries.title: NPM - step-by-step exploitation scenario
entries.body:
Scenario using npm as an example:

1. List all packages from the manifests (package.json, composer.json, ...).
2. Find a package that is not on www.npmjs.com (absent from the public registry - a substitution candidate).
3. Register and create a PUBLIC package with the same name.

Example exploit package: 0xsapra/dependency-confusion-expoit (github.com/0xsapra/dependency-confusion-expoit).

entries.title: NPM — the execution primitive (preinstall/postinstall)
Tools for finding dependency confusion
entries.body:
- visma-prodsec/confused - checks for dependency confusion vulnerabilities across several package management systems (github.com/visma-prodsec/confused).
- synacktiv/DepFuzzer - finds dependency confusion or projects where you can take over the package owner's email (github.com/synacktiv/DepFuzzer).
